Off Calendar Mar-30-2020 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Nov-15-2019 Continued To Mar-30-20 At 08:00 A.M. In Dept 306
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Cynthia J. Larsen, Esq., counsel for California Insurance Commissioner; Todd Padnos, Esq., counsel for Liberty Mutual Insurance Co.

Court and counsel discuss the status of the case and how best to proceed to resolution. Ms. Larsen reports that the plan proposed by Respondent Liberty Mutual has been reviewed the Insurance Commissioner's representatives and while other departments within the Commission would require review, she and Mr. Padnos believe that a fully vetted plan can be filed by March 2020. Mr. Padnos concurs and provides the Court with further edification on the general operation of the plan which, generally speaking, is modeled after a bankruptcy plan of re-organization but provide protection to his client, preserving various rights it accrued from the State of California upon assuming the liabilities of the now-defunct Golden Eagle Ins. Co.

Court and counsel discuss the efficacies of going forward with the plan or maintaining the status quo, to wit: annual case management conferences. Mr. Padnos expresses no preference. Ms. Larsen advises the Court that her client would realize both time and economic efficiencies by proceeding with the plan. The Court determines to go forward with the plan.

After further discussion, the Court determines that the Motion to Approve the Plan shall be filed by March 30, 2020 and sets hearing on the motion for May 1, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. At the Court's inquiry, Ms. Larsen generally advises the Court on the scope of service for the motion and notes that the time for service is standard notice service.

Court adjourns.

Judge: Hon. Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D306)

Off Calendar Sep-07-2018 Text Ruling

Status Conference.
Off Calendar Dec-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Welsh for Plaintiff California Insurance Commissioner; Todd Padnos for Defendant Liberty Mutual Ins. Co.

Court and counsel briefly review a synopsis of the proposed plan which, generally speaking, would call for a motion to dismiss and all remaining claims to be addressed administratively. In the absence of administrative resolution, the parties would be authorized to re-open the existing case for resolution by the court. Mr. Welsh represresents that, given the holidays, he anticipates completing the moving pleadings shortly after the new year and will schedule a hearing on the same with the clerk. The Court generally finds the synopsis acceptable in principal but will await receipt of the motion(s) and supporting pleadings.

Judge: Hon. Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; not reported. (D306)

Status Conference.
Off Calendar Dec-04-2017 Continued To Dec-21-17 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 306

Status Conference.
Off Calendar Nov-13-2017 Continued To Dec-14-17 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 306

Status Conference.
Off Calendar Sep-22-2017 Continued To Nov-16-17 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 306
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Welsh for Plaintiff California Insurance Commissioner; Todd Padnos for Defendant Liberty Mutual Ins. Co.

Court and counsel review status of pending liabilities of the receiver, noting actuarial estimates of approximately $53-54 million likely outstanding claims with re-insurance coverage of $136 million which counsel believe should be sufficient to cover future liabilities and anomalies.

Mr. Welsh reports on the status of Mr. Padnos' plan to administratively "close" the case, reserving jurisdiction to the Court to re-open the case for extraordinary issues. Counsel believe that the final terms of the plan will be term-sheeted within the next several weeks but notes that, among other significant elements, it contemplates motion to dismiss in which the order for same would incorporate a series of injunctions generally authorized by Insurance Code Sec. 1020. Additionally, a trust would be set up to administer a $1 million aministrative expense fund.

The Court continues the status conference to November 16, 2017 at 1:30 p.m. whereupon counsel will submit formal written plan for finalizing the administration of the case.

Judge: Hon. Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; not reported. (D306)

Status Conference.
Off Calendar Jun-20-2017 Continued To Sep-22-17 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 306
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Welsh for Plaintiff California Insurance Commissioner; Todd Padnos for Defendant Liberty Mutual Ins. Co.

Mr. Welsh reports on the status, noting new "pollution" claims arising under old legacy policies, and notes in general terms Mr. Padnos' plan to administratively "close" the case, reserving jurisdiction to the Court to re-open the case under special circumstances.

The Court continues the status conference to Sept. 22, 2017 at 1:30 p.m. with an eye toward submitting a formal written plan for finalizing the administration of the case.

Judge: Hon. Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; not reported. (D306)
